fact: August 26 sits exactly 238 days into the year - that's like counting every single day since New Year's and then adding 69 more!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when many spiders start getting super busy building their webs because they know autumn is coming and they need to catch lots of bugs before winter.
The sun rises about 2 minutes later than it did just one week ago - it's like the Earth is hitting the snooze button on summer!
Why do leaves start changing colors right around now in some places? Because trees are basically getting ready to go to sleep for winter and they stop making the green stuff called chlorophyll.
August 26 falls smack in the middle of Virgo season, which means if the zodiac were a pizza, we'd be right in the perfectionist slice that loves organizing everything.
Monarch butterflies in North America are starting their epic 3,000-mile journey south right about now - imagine flying from Canada to Mexico on wings thinner than paper!
This date sits exactly 58 days before the autumn equinox, when day and night become perfectly equal like a cosmic balance scale.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, August 26 is deep winter, so while kids in America are sweating, Australian kids might be building snowmen!
Crickets chirp slower as temperatures drop, so by late August their nighttime concerts sound different than they did in July - nature's own thermometer!
The moon phase on August 26 changes every year, but when it's a new moon on this date, you can see way more stars because there's no bright moon washing them out.
Squirrels go absolutely nuts around this time of year, frantically collecting and burying acorns - a single squirrel can bury up to 10,000 nuts before winter!
Morning dew gets heavier and sticks around longer in late August because the air holds more moisture as temperatures start their slow dance toward fall.
